title: Exercises: Quantum Cloud API Access and Authentication

exercises.title: Multi-Provider Authentication Setup
exercises.description: Implement a unified credential manager that handles authentication for multiple quantum cloud providers.
tasks: Create a CredentialManager class that stores credentials for IBM, Braket, and Google
Implement load_from_environment() to read credentials from environment variables
Implement load_from_file() to read from provider-specific config files
Add credential validation for each provider's format
Implement get_client() that returns an authenticated client for a given provider
exercises.expectedOutput: Working credential manager with multi-provider support

exercises.title: Rate Limiter Implementation
exercises.description: Build a robust rate limiter with sliding window and burst handling.
tasks: Implement sliding window rate limiting (not fixed windows)
Add support for burst allowance above base rate
Track rate limits per-endpoint (different limits for different API calls)
Implement async-compatible waiting mechanism
Add metrics collection for rate limit events
exercises.expectedOutput: Sliding window rate limiter with burst support and per-endpoint limits
hints: Use deque for efficient sliding window
Burst allows temporary spikes above base rate
Track oldest request to calculate wait time

exercises.title: Retry Decorator with Backoff Strategies
exercises.description: Create a flexible retry decorator supporting multiple backoff strategies.
tasks: Implement exponential backoff strategy
Implement linear backoff strategy
Implement fibonacci backoff strategy
Add jitter to prevent thundering herd
Support configurable retry conditions (which exceptions to retry)
Add callback hooks for retry events
exercises.expectedOutput: Flexible retry decorator with multiple backoff strategies

exercises.title: Secure Credential Storage
exercises.description: Implement encrypted credential storage using industry-standard encryption.
tasks: Use Fernet symmetric encryption (from cryptography library)
Derive encryption key from user-provided password using PBKDF2
Store encrypted credentials with integrity verification
Implement credential rotation with old key decryption
Add expiration timestamps for credentials
exercises.starterCode:
import os
import json
import hashlib
import base64
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, Optional
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

# Note: In production, use 'from cryptography.fernet import Fernet'
# This exercise simulates the pattern

@dataclass
class EncryptedCredential:
    provider: str
    encrypted_data: bytes
    salt: bytes
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: Optional[datetime]

class SecureCredentialStore:
    def __init__(self, storage_path: str):
        self.storage_path = storage_path
        self._master_key: Optional[bytes] = None
    
    def initialize(self, password: str) -> None:
        """Initialize store with master password."""
        pass
    
    def _derive_key(self, password: str, salt: bytes) -> bytes:
        """Derive encryption key using PBKDF2."""
        pass
    
    def store_credential(self, provider: str, credential: Dict,
                        expires_in_days: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
        """Store encrypted credential."""
        pass
    
    def retrieve_credential(self, provider: str) -> Dict:
        """Retrieve and decrypt credential."""
        pass
    
    def rotate_credential(self, provider: str, new_credential: Dict,
                         new_password: Optional[str] = None) -> None:
        """Rotate credential, optionally with new password."""
        pass
    
    def check_expiration(self, provider: str) -> bool:
        """Check if credential is expired."""
        pass
exercises.expectedOutput: Secure encrypted credential store with key derivation and rotation
hints: PBKDF2 iterations should be at least 100,000
